message:
🌱 improve: rewrite /from-lens with collaborative tone and fix inaccuracies (#2913)

The /from-lens page had a 50% bounce rate (vs 34% for /from-headlamp)
partly due to its adversarial tone and several factual inaccuracies:

Tone changes:
- "Switching from Lens?" → "Coming from Lens?"
- "No account. No subscription." → "Lens is a solid Kubernetes IDE.
  Console adds multi-cluster AI, GPU visibility, and built-in ops."
- "Everything Lens had. Plus everything it didn't." → "What Console
  adds to your toolkit"
- "Ready to switch?" → "Ready to explore?"
- Added Lens acknowledgment section (like /from-headlamp has)
- Red comparison colors → neutral slate for Lens column

Factual corrections:
- "Account Required: Yes" → "For Pro features" (Lens Desktop is free)
- "Price: $199/year" → removed (misleading, Lens Desktop is free)
- "AI Assistance: No" → "Lens AI (Pro)" (Lens has AI since 2024)
- "Telemetry: Required" → removed (Lens telemetry is configurable)
- Added "Desktop App" row where Lens legitimately wins
- Added "CNCF Tool Cards" row for Console differentiation
- "Lens Pro" column header → "Lens"

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:

{ feature: 'Security Posture', lens: false, console: true, consoleNote: 'Built-in' }, + { feature: 'Security Posture', lens: 'Via extensions', console: true, consoleNote: 'Built-in' }, { feature: 'Cost Analytics', lens: false, console: true, consoleNote: 'Built-in (OpenCost)' }, { feature: 'GitOps Status', lens: false, console: true, consoleNote: 'Built-in (ArgoCD/Flux)' }, + { feature: 'CNCF Tool Cards', lens: false, console: true, consoleNote: 'KEDA, Strimzi, KubeVela, etc.' }, ] /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ @@ -218,7 +218,7 @@ function ComparisonCell({ value, note, isConsole }: { value: string | boolean; n return ( - Lens does a lot of things right

+

+ Lens pioneered the desktop Kubernetes IDE experience with its Electron app, rich extension ecosystem, and clean resource browser. + If Lens works well for your team, keep using it! KubeStellar Console is designed for teams that need + AI-powered troubleshooting, multi-cluster management at scale, GPU/AI-ML workload visibility, and + built-in cost and security analytics — capabilities that complement what Lens provides. +

+ + Visit k8slens.dev + + +
+
+ {/* ---- Feature Highlights ---- */}

- Everything Lens had.{' '} - Plus everything it didn't. + What Console{' '} + adds to your toolkit

- KubeStellar Console goes beyond basic cluster management with AI, security, cost, and GitOps built in. + Built-in capabilities that go beyond single-cluster resource browsing.
